component.name: godotGem
component.summary: Controller client to emulate an xbox360 controller for windows

description.p: Turn your steam deck into a windows controller (Or use any controller from a Linux PC on windows). Connect linux to windows over the network in order to bind an emulated controller on windows to the same inputs you're doing on linux. Useful for playing games that might lack support for your controller out of the box, or just need another controller despite running out of USB ports

ul.li: DualSense
Dualshock 4
Logitech Gamepad F310
Nintendo Switch Pro Controller
Steam Controller
Steam Deck
Xbox One (+ series) controller
Your keyboard (See: Options -> Other -> Key To Controller)

description.p: Other controllers likely work too as long as the godot game engine supports it.

ul.li: Chroma Backgrounds
Change the color of your controller
Connect multiple clients to one server, switch between player 1 - 4 [Beta]
Click / Tap a button to activate turbo on that button
Press F1 to hide the UI
Frame Rate Limiting
Blinder
Button Heat Mapping

description.p: Note: you will need to install ViGEm and the godotGem server on windows. (See releases section from Github, linked below)
It's been a while... hello there! An entirely new app is ready, right at your fingertips. A lot has changed and their's more to come. Here's what we have:

ul.li: UI Overhaul
Turbo mode for each button (select one by clicking / tapping!)
RGB Background, Green Screen BG, Magenta Screen BG (for streamers!)
Gamepad Redsign, actually looks Like a controller
An Options Tab
Feedback toggle for turbo
Change which player you are! (Players 1-4) [Beta]
Change the color of your controller!
Button heat map (see how often you press a button)
Keyboard to controller! Press a key on the keyboard, and it translates to a controller input.
Change the connection port (99.9% won't need this)
Frame Rate Limiter (FPS: 15, 30, 60, uncapped)
Press F1 on the keyboard to hide the UI
Blinder stops everything from rendering

description.p: That's everything! If you have questions or need to troubleshoot, check out discord server via the github project

ul.li: New feature! You can can now use a bridge to communicate outside a Local area network. It's a middle man between a client and server. Check the docs(extras/bridgeHowTo.md) to find out more

Client - vibrations coming back from the server can now be ignored through a toggle. This was added so that the dualSense doesn't go into legacy vibration mode if you're routing audio to the controller from elsewhere.
Server - a bridge mode was added, which effectively doubled the code size. Also vibrations from the emulated controller go to all clients instead of one.
Bridge + Server - optimized stability when disconnecting and reconnecting
Multiple godotGemClients can now be used on the server at once
Fixed a bug where the server would crash if you tried to re-connect after a client crash. (Or if the client didn't send a disconnect signal)
The client now matches the resolution of the steam deck display by default
The UI in the client is now larger and more organized
